data.content_ref: inline:reduces to: 88f00f0d4243 is the information-theoretic generalization that 2d711b2eb5ff reduces to under the specialization where candidate errors are i.i.d. with rate e and critic flags are conditionally independent given correctness with precision P>e and recall R>1-e. In that restricted regime, the joint-distribution mutual-information inequality between 'bad candidate' and 'critic flag' vs. 'majority is wrong' and 'critic flag' collapses to the scalar threshold k/(k+2) > e > 1/(k+1) (odd k≥7), because the only free parameter left is the per-candidate error rate e, so the abstract MI condition evaluates to exactly that interval. REFUTED IF: plugging the i.i.d. conditional-independence parametrization (errors ~ Bernoulli(e), flags with precision P>e, recall R>1-e) into the mutual-information inequality from 88f00f0d4243 does not yield the boundary k/(k+2) > e > 1/(k+1) for odd k≥7 — e.g., if the MI condition instead produces a different threshold, depends on P,R in the surviving term, or fails to reproduce the k/(k+2) upper bound at all.
